Another one will be probably, and so these first two, I'm not sure if both of these will happen right now, but it will probably be either just pure out recording and or pure recording with Q&A calls that are just with, with John and Mark or coaches, my, my Platinum level coaches. And then maybe once a year, I'll do it live and they'll be like calls, calls with me, but the pricing for that will be way higher, like maybe, you know, five times as high. So again, it's totally MSU. You can make this up any way you want. You know, the way I've done PLF over the years is I've often done live calls with PLF, it keeps me sharp and, and it provides tremendous value, but going forward, my schedule just gets crazier and crazier. And I think I'm going to move away from doing those live calls or only do like live calls a few times a year. But again, you completely make up the rules yourself. You know, obviously, if you're doing live Q&A, it's, it's got a, it's generally going to have a higher perceived value and it might have an actual real higher value. But I mean, if you just did like, you know, four or five awesome Q&A calls, and maybe you just, who knows, maybe you went and edited them. So you just took the best questions of the five calls and edited it down to like now there's, instead of there being five calls that were an hour and a half each. So you got like seven and a half hours of calls. If you edited those down and just put the best questions together and people got the best 60 or 90 minutes, you could argue that that would be better than doing live calls. No, either way, you can completely just define it how you want. It's MSU. You make stuff up. You, you make your rules. Just know that the way you change the rules around can change the perceived value and can change the pricing. Okay. So that slide pretty clear. Everyone got that? Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. That's what the seed launch can do for you. That's what your seed launch will do. So I just have one final thought here. And this is a quote that I absolutely love. And I know many of you have seen this and many of you are familiar with this. But it's from Marianne Williamson. And this is one of her, she wrote this. Our deepest fears not that we are inadequate. Our deepest fears that we are powerful beyond measure. It is our light, not our darkness that most frightens us. We ask ourselves, who am I to be brilliant, gorgeous, talented, fabulous? Actually, who are you not to be? You're a child of God. Your plain small does not serve the world. There's nothing enlightened about shrinking so that other people won't feel insecure around you.

## [1:09:30 - 1:12:33]

We are all meant to shine as children do. We are born to make manifest the glory of God that is within us. It is not just in some of us, it's in everyone. And as we let our light shine, we unconsciously give other people permission to do the same. As we are liberated from our own fear, our presence automatically liberates others. So who are you to hold yourself back? Who are you to play small? Do you think that sometimes I have those thoughts in my life? Do you think that possibly sometimes I say, who the hell am I? Did you hear Dory say that? That she or his times were said, who the hell am I to be teaching this? When I stand up on stage, do you think everything the thought goes through my head, who the hell am I to be up here telling people what to do with their lives and how to live? Well, I'll tell you what, that thought goes through my head all the freaking time. It goes through all of our heads. But the reality is that you have something to bring to the world. I have something to bring to the world. I've brought it to the world, but I still have to step up every day. Every day I sit down at my desk and I've got to make the decision, am I going to step up and play big or am I going to play small? Am I going to let that perfectionism, that feeling that I have to be perfect, am I going to use that as an excuse to procrastinate and not bring my light to the world, not step up, not play a bigger game? You know, I'm not perfect. Sometimes I do. Sometimes I choose that perfectionism. Sometimes I sit there and I choose to just check my email and check Facebook all day long. But every time I do that, I'm choosing to play small. And that's not the game I'm in. And I don't think that's the game you're in. So who are you to hold yourself back? It's time to step up. It's time to remember those three letters, that MSU. You make this game up the way that it works for you. You get to make up the rules. You get to define the rules. So make up rules that you're going to win. It's time to move. It's not about the camera. It's not about which camera are you going to shoot video with. Now is your moment. Now is your time. This process I've taught you works. It's worked over and over. It worked for me. It's the very basis of what I've built a $20 million business with. It's the very basis of what I've been able to build a team of now nine or 10 people that I get to work with.

## [1:12:33 - 1:13:38]

That I get to tap into their genius. I've built that all off of the C-Bodge. It's how I've literally changed an industry from selling six people into my C-Bodge. Who are you to play small? Who are you to hold back? Now is your time. It's not about the camera. Now is the time to step up. So thank you for sticking with me. I've loved this course. I've loved teaching you. I've loved the interaction. As I mentioned, we're going to have more bonuses coming up. But for now, go get them. And remember to tell us about your C-Lunch. I want to hear about it. My whole team wants to hear about it. Thanks a lot, everyone. Goodbye. Thank you.
